results.topBearish: #Spain BANS #Palantir From Every State Company - 3rd Country in 6 Weeks, #Europe PURGING #USA #CdnPoli #PQPoli #ONPoli #TOPoli #NSPoli #BCPoli #ABPoli #SKPoli #UKPol #EUPol #EUPoli #AUPol #AUSPol #NSWPol #NZPol #JAPPol #MXPoli #INDPol #SAPol #SAPolitics
results.summary: Sentiment around $PLTR is predominantly negative, driven by a wave of geopolitical backlash as European nations ban Palantir from state contracts, alongside persistent civil liberties and AI ethics controversies that risk reputational and revenue damage. However, a cluster of options sweep alerts and a defined technical buy zone suggest some traders anticipate a near-term price move, though the macro headwinds from international regulatory resistance represent a meaningful competitive threat.

results.name: Amazon
results.topBullish: @BillAckman —These investments will yield highly attractive returns, particularly for hyperscalers like $AMZN because they provide the most scalable access to the immense compute power AI demands. He also thinks $META is cheap at current levels.
results.topBearish: @bjmtweets Obviously $AMZN has a larger TAM, but the question is how much room either has to expand relative to the price you're paying now. I think it's much more likely that $MELI becomes a 300-400 billion dollar company than $AMZN becoming a 12 trillion dollar company.
results.summary: Social media sentiment around $AMZN is broadly neutral to slightly positive, dominated by Amazon Prime Day activity, streaming content mentions, and scattered retail grievances around Prime subscription value. Institutional-level bullish signals center on AWS positioning as a leading AI hyperscaler, though skeptics question AMZN's remaining upside at current valuations relative to smaller, faster-growing peers like MELI.

results.summary: Social media discussion around $KEEL is overwhelmingly dominated by repetitive WhatsApp pump-group spam, severely degrading signal quality and raising manipulation concerns. Stripping out spam, genuine sentiment is mildly bearish: a Nasdaq deficiency letter, a 15%+ single-day drop in Canada, CEO option expiration extensions, and inclusion in weekly Russell 2000 loser lists all point to near-term downside risk despite a notable six-month rally from lows.

results.name: Applied Digital
keyThemes: Insider retention of performance shares as bullish signal
High spam/signal-service noise diluting organic sentiment
results.topBullish: Applied Digital plans $3.6 Billion expansion in Alexandria Louisiana creating 200 new jobs.
results.topBearish: @StockOptionCole Only if it comes with a refund for $APLD haha
results.summary: Social sentiment around $APLD is moderately bullish, anchored by a concrete $3.6B data center expansion announcement in Louisiana and insider confidence signals — notably the President retaining 61% of vested performance shares with zero open-market sales alongside a 200MW capacity target by late 2025. However, sentiment quality is significantly diluted by repetitive spam posts (analyst target links, WhatsApp/Discord solicitations, and signal-service promotions), making organic conviction harder to gauge and keeping confidence at medium.

results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: Stock down ~44% from ATH amid AI sector bear market
Missed AI revenue targets for two consecutive quarters
results.topBullish:
5 stocks I think are getting way too cheap to ignore 👀

Everyone wants to buy when stocks are ripping. I'd rather build positions when the chart gets boring, sentiment gets ugly, and the business keeps improving underneath the surface.

The 5 I'm watching closely:
$IREN
$NVDA
results.topBearish: Crickets on the RSUs, of course. Wouldn't touch $IREN with a 10 foot pole right now. Not sure they've even accomplished much to warrant the current price. And I've been in since the mid-teens..
results.summary: Iris Energy sentiment is deeply split: long-term bulls cite AI compute demand and a compelling valuation after a ~44% drawdown from ATH, while a vocal cohort of shareholders is incensed by an $800M time-based RSU compensation package representing ~5% dilution and two consecutive quarters of missed AI revenue targets. Management credibility is under significant pressure, with repeated calls to remove a named executive seen as damaging the company's reputation and potentially creating legal exposure.

results.name: Tesla
results.topBullish: Tesla's new Semi factory is massive… 1.7 million sq ft, aiming for 50,000 trucks a year. Ashlee Vance gets the full tour. Insane scale. $TSLA #Tesla #Semi #EVManufacturing
results.topBearish: BREAKING🚨: We are about to witness either one of the BIGGEST FLOPS or GREATEST TRADES of all time…🍿👀 Michael Burry is officially back in "Big Short" mode after unveiling FIVE new short positions against major AI-related stocks…😳👇 Nvidia $NVDA: Tesla $TSLA Micron $MU
results.summary: Sentiment around TSLA is cautiously optimistic, driven by real-world Robotaxi deployment in Miami, the massive Semi factory ramp, and bullish technical setups targeting the $445–$455 range, though notable headwinds include Michael Burry's short position and key support risk around the $368–$380 zone. The post mix is diluted by spam, scam giveaway bots, and promotional content, reducing overall signal quality and warranting a measured NEUTRAL stance rather than a directional conviction call.

results.name: First Trust Cybersecurity ETF
keyThemes: Steady fund flows with low retail attention
Enterprise spending resilience in risk-off environment
Broadening market leadership into defense-tilted thematic funds
results.topBullish: $CIBR has pulled in steady flows all year and nobody talks about it. Cybersecurity is one of the few sectors where enterprise budget cuts stall but never reverse. Breach costs keep rising. That theme does not go away in a risk off tape.
results.summary: Sentiment around $CIBR is decidedly bullish, driven by consistent fund inflows, relative outperformance versus broader innovation ETFs, and strong momentum in key holdings like PANW, CRWD, and RBRK. The cybersecurity sector's structural demand tailwinds — rising breach costs and non-discretionary enterprise budgets — are resonating with investors seeking defensive growth exposure in a risk-off environment.
